items.mistake: Using print() everywhere for diagnostics in an application.
items.fix: Use logging with levels so output can be filtered and routed centrally.
items.why: print has no severity, no routing, and can't be silenced per component.
items.example:
```python
# BAD
print('starting')
print('done')

# GOOD
log.info('starting')
log.info('done')
# operators can now set LOG_LEVEL=WARNING to silence the noise
```

items.why: f-strings build the string even when the message is below the active level.
items.example:
```python
# BAD: format runs even if DEBUG is off
log.debug(f'embedding chunk {chunk!r}')

# GOOD: format only if level is enabled
log.debug('embedding chunk %r', chunk)
```

items.mistake: Logging full prompts, responses, or API keys.
items.fix: Log identifiers, counts and truncated/redacted content.
items.why: Logs are often retained and widely readable; secrets and PII must not land there.
items.example:
```python
# BAD
log.info('prompt: %s', prompt)        # may contain user PII
# GOOD
log.info('prompt_id=%s tokens=%d', pid, n)
```

items.mistake: Catching an exception and logging only str(e).
items.fix: Use log.exception(...) inside the except block.
items.why: log.exception includes the full traceback, which is essential for debugging.
